title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: They think that the building was abandoned over fifty years ago.
questions.explanation: Tests complex passive with perfect infinitive (is thought to have + been).

questions.firstSentence: ‘I'm sorry I didn't get back to you sooner,’ said the manager.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verb 'apologise' + preposition 'for' + negative gerund.

questions.firstSentence: The match was cancelled because it rained heavily.

routes.p2: not have been cancelled but for

questions.explanation: Tests conditional meaning using 'but for' (meaning 'if it hadn't been for').

questions.firstSentence: I'm sure that the car didn't just disappear on its own.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for negative deduction in the past.

questions.firstSentence: He had only just arrived at the office when the alarm went off.
questions.afterGap: at the office when the alarm went off.
questions.explanation: Tests inversion with the restrictive adverb 'Hardly'.

questions.firstSentence: I haven't seen a movie as good as this one for a long time.
questions.explanation: Tests 'It is [time] since...' structures with present perfect or past simple.
